revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:穂積五百枝]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|father={{ill|Hozumi no Kujii|qid=Q22129343|1=Hozumi no Kujii}}|relatives={{ill|Hozumi no Momotari|qid=Q11597083|1=Hozumi no Momotari}}}}
'''Hozumi no Ioe''' (穂積 五百枝) was a figure from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} of Japan. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|qid=Q1664099|lt=kabane|1=Kabane}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|qid=Q2574718|lt=Omi|1=Omi (title)}}. He is listed in genealogies as the son of {{ill|Hozumi no Kujii|ja|穂積咋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Kujii|qq=}}<ref>「穂積系図」 (Ōta Ryō, *Sei Shi Kakei Daijiten*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1963)</ref>. During the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|qid=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}} of {{ill|672|en|672|qid=Q30601|lt=672|1=672}}, he served as a messenger mobilizing troops for Emperor Kōbun (Prince Ōtomo) but ultimately defected to Prince Ōama (later Emperor Tenmu) after a coup.
The name appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} as both "百枝" and "五百枝," with the former read as "Momoe." Some scholars suggest "五百枝" is the correct form, as "五" might have been omitted accidentally.
== Career == In the Jinshin year, when Prince Ōama launched his rebellion, the court at {{ill|Ōmi Ōtsu Palace|en|Ōmi Ōtsu Palace|qid=Q11638487|lt=Ōmi Ōtsu Palace|1=Ōmi Ōtsu Palace}} sent emissaries to mobilize armies for suppression. Hozumi no Ioe, along with {{ill|Hozumi no Momotari|lt=Hozumi no Momotari|lt=Hozumi no Momochi|ja|穂積百足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Momochi|qq=}} (his brother) and {{ill|Mononobe no Hinuka|ja|物部日向|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Hinuka|1=mononobe no himuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11570695}}, was dispatched to the Yamato region to lead forces from the old capital of {{ill|Asuka|ja|飛鳥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka|qq=}}. They collaborated with the governor, Prince Takasaka, and began assembling an army near the {{ill|Asuka-dera|en|Asuka-dera|qid=Q779755|lt=Asuka-dera|1=Asuka-dera}}, west of a large zelkova tree.
Meanwhile, in Yamato, {{ill|Ōtomo no Fukei|ja|大伴吹負|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Fukei|qq=}} was gathering allies for Prince Ōama. Fukei conspired with another official, {{ill|Sakagami no Kumake|ja|坂上熊毛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakagami no Kumake|qq=}}, to claim he was acting on behalf of Prince Takechi, who was supposedly organizing forces in the Mino Province for Ōama. Their strategy involved Fukei approaching the army camp while Kumake staged an internal rebellion.
On the 29th day of the 6th month, Fukei entered the camp west of Asuka-dera and successfully seized command, aided by Kumake's betrayal. Hozumi no Ioe and Mononobe no Hinuka were unable to resist and were taken captive. Hozumi no Momochi, summoned to the camp, was executed. Ioe and Hinuka were later pardoned and joined Prince Ōama's forces.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Kotobank}}
